adj. & adv. How many? &c. ( = kittā, kitnā, q.v.).

adj. & s.f. Having a waist like a lion, slim-waisted; — a slim-waisted woman.

def: s.m. A writer, a scribe, an amanuensis, a copier, a clerk.

s.m. Striking the beak (into), pecking; seizing with the beak

def: The febrifuge nut Guilandina bonducella.

subs: or kaṭkana karnā (-ko), To sub-let, to farm out
To set a copy of skeleton letters.

v.t. = kāṭ-khānā, q.v.s.v. kāṭnā.
s.m. = kaṭkanā, q.v.

s.f. Writing-chalk, a species of steatites (used to write with, and in polishing stucco).

ety: kat, for kad = kada, qq.v.+ḵẖudā, q.v.

s.f. Marriage; establishing, or managing, a family; state of a householder; housekeeping; — the office of viceroy
s.m. Killing, slaying, slaughtering; putting to death, executing, execution; — murder; homicide; assassination; — bloodshed, slaughter, carnage, massacre
subs: or qatl-ě-ḵẖat̤ā, Killing by mistake; accidental homicide; manslaughter
v.t. To kill, slay; to put to death, to execute; to murder, to assassinate; to slaughter, massacre
or qatl-gah, s.f. Place of execution; place or scene of murder or carnage
v.n. To be killed; to be put to death, &c.

ety: for کتلا katlā = کترا katrā, fr. katarnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A slice (cf. Eng. cutlet).

ety: prob. = katrā, q.v.; and cf. qatlā
def: s.m. A slice
adj. & s.m. Very stout and lusty; burly and rude, sturdy and overbearing; — a stout and lusty fellow; a lusty, indolent fellow, &c. (also written kaṭh-mastā).
adj. & s.m. Very stout and lusty; burly and rude, sturdy and overbearing; — a stout and lusty fellow; a lusty, indolent fellow, &c. (also written kaṭh-mastā).
s.m. A hedge-schoolmaster; — a great bigot (also kath-mullāṅ).

ety: kāt˚ = Prk. कत्त(इ) or कत्ते(इ); S. कर्तय(ति), caus. of rt. कृत् 'to spin'
def: v.t. To spin (cotton or thread); to twist or wrap round.

ety: kāṭ˚ = Prk. कट्ट(इ) or कट्टे(इ); S. कर्तय(ति), caus. of rt. कृत्
def: v.t. To cut, clip, lop, prune; to hack, slash, cut asunder, sever, disjoin, disunite, divide; to dissect; to saw; to cleave; to carve, to chisel; to mow, to reap; to clear (land of jungle); to bite; to nip, to sting; to cut or wound with satire; to carp at, find fault with; to make ashamed, to shame, to put to the blush, to confuse; to cut away, to erode; to corrode; to cut out, mark or lay out (a road); to cut out, strike out, erase; to cancel; to exclude; to pass, spend, or while away (time); to waste (time); to tarry, stop, stay, remain; to traverse, or cross, or get over (a road); to extract (colour from anything); to interrupt, or intercept (speech); to refute, rebut; to put an end to, settle, or adjust (a dispute, &c.); to deduct; to liberate; to earn, or gain (money)
subs: (-ko), To cut off; to bite off
v.t. (intens.) To cut off, lop off, strike off, chop off, amputate; to cut down, to hew, &c.
v.t. (intens.), To cut off; to bite off, &c.

def: v.n. To be spun.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀬 (kittiya) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀦- (-na-), from Sanskrit *कियत्त (*kiyatta), variant of कियत् (kíyat). Doublet of کِتَّا (kittā). Cognate with Punjabi ਕਿਤਨਾ /کِتْنا (kitnā).

def: v.n. To be cut, be clipped; to be reaped; to be cut off, be amputated; to be killed (in fight); — to be retrenched, to be diminished; to be deducted (from); to be spent, to be squandered; to be spent or passed (as time, life, &c.); to cease, to come to an end, be put an end to; to be interrupted; to pass away; to disappear, vanish; to dissolve, melt (as snow, ice, &c.); to be wiped or blotted out, be effaced or obliterated, be forgiven (as sins); — to come out, to be extracted (as colour from cloth, &c.); — to be ashamed, be abashed; to be consumed with jealousy, &c.
subs: v.n. To be cut off, be severed or sundered, be intercepted, be divided, be separated (from); to be stopped up (as a road infested with robbers, &c.); — to be spent, be consumed, be squandered, &c.
To cut and run, to slink away.

def: s.f. = katāʼī, q.v
s.m. A species of bird, Coracias.
s.f. Cutting; cutting down; reaping; cutting (a pack (of cards)

s.f. Cutting, chipping, paring, &c.; excision; a piece; cuttings, clippings, parings (syn. ćhāṅṭan).
s.m. Liquid pitch; tar; the juice of the pine.

v.t. To cause to cut or clip; to cut out; — v.n. To move or go aside, to get out of the way (of, -se), to slink away (from); to leave the high road and steal away by a by-path; to go round about, to take a round; to edge or go sideways or zigzag
s.f. = kaṭhrī, q.v.

ety: rt. of katar(nā)+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A cutter, &c.

ety: inf. n. of قطّ 'to cut transversely,' &c.
def: s.m. Cutting (a thing) transversely, sideways, or across; cutting or making a pen, cutting the nib of a pen; — the nib of a pen
subs: or qat̤-gīr, s.m. A pin or peg, or a flat piece of bone or stick, on which writing-reeds or pens are cut or nibbed
(-ko), To cut or nib a writing-reed or pen.

def: s.m. A large fat louse.

ety: prob. S. ग्रन्थित+कः; cf. gaṭhā, perf. part. of gaṭhnā; — and also gaṭṭā, and gaṭṭhā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-knit, able-bodied, stout, strong, sturdy, robust, athletie; hard, stiff; firm, fixed, determined; mortal, deadly (as hate, or a foe); strict, thorough-going, unshakeable, sound; — s.m. (dialec.), A bundle, a faggot, &c. ( = gaṭṭhā, q.v.).
s.m. = ghaṭṭa, or ghāṭ, q.v.
s.m. A great slayer; a slayer, killer, murderer.
s.f. A chip, a shred, strip; a piece of stone, or brick, or earth, a shard
subs: s.m. Throwing a shard, &c. so as to make it skim along the surface of water; the game of ducks and drakes (syn. ćhićhilī).

ety: for A. qat̤t̤āmat, intens. n. fem. fr. the inf. n. قطم qat̤am, 'having a strong desire for coition'
def: s.f. A lascivious woman, a strumpet, an adulteress; a term of abuse.
s.m. (dialec.) A knife, dagger.
adj. & s.m. Addicted to biting (a horse, &c.); — a biter.
s.f. The horizontal beam to which the bullocks are attached in a sugar-mill ( = kaṭhrī).
s.f. Land recovered from, or left by, large rivers.
s.m. A measure of land, the twentieth part of a bīghā of 1600 sq. yds.; — a corn measure of five sers.

ety: S. क्कथः, or क्काथः; and क्कथ+ कः
def: s.m. An astringent vegetable extract (the produce of the Mimosa catechu) which the natives of India eat with betel leaf; catechu, terra japonica (syn. khair, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Boastful; — a boaster; — the act of boasting, boast.
s.f. A small sword, a scimitar.
s.f. (old Hindī), A small sword, &c. ( = kattī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Cutting; biting; pungent; corrosive; — a cutter, cleaver, &c.

adj. Pungent, acrid, sharp, cutting, biting; bitter, caustic; disagreeable; fierce, hot, vehement, impetuous; — s.m. Pungency (of taste, smell, or speech), acerbity, &c.; — the flower Michelia campaca.
s.m. per met. for takuʼā, q.v.
adj. = کٿو kaṭu, q.v.

ety: S. कटुक+sī, fem. of sā, 'like,' q.v.
def: s.f. Foul language, abuse, smut, obscenity, scurrility.
s.f. Pungency, sharpness; bitterness; keenness; impetuosity, vehemence; irritability, testiness; wickedness.
s.m. The man who guides and draws out the (leather) bucket from the well.
s.f. Price paid for spinning ( = katāʼī).
adj. Cut or ripped up, split up; deducted; simple (interest).
v.t. To cause to be spun, to have or get spun.
v.t. To cause to be cut, or cut up, or cut off; to have (a thing) cut, or cut off, &c.; to cause to be bitten.
s.m. corr. of kotwāl, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. qat̤t̤ or qat̤.
adv. (Braj & Dakkhinī) = kab, q.v.
ex: Please give some water to my elder brother.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کوں (kvṉ /⁠kū̃⁠/)., from Old Hindi काहू (kāhū), काहु (kāhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit कक्षे (kákṣe, “in the armpit”), locative singular of कक्ष (kákṣa, “armpit”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *káṭṣas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *káćšas, from Proto-Indo-European *kóḱsos (“joint”). Compare Deccani کو (kū), Braj कौ (kau), Assamese -ক (-k). The semantic development of the terms w…

def: adv. (Braj) = kab-hu, or kab-hī, q.v.

ety: prob. a dialec. form of koćnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To stow away, to pocket, to steal.
s.m. = kauṅdā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Slow, stupid, dull, thick-headed, weak in mind; — a stupid person, a dolt, simpleton; — a mean or sorry horse; a pack-horse.
s.f. Dulness of mind, obtuseness, stupidity.
v.n. = kauṅdnā, or kauṅdhnā, q.v.
s.m. A star; constellation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کوکب (kawkab), from Arabic كَوْكَب (kawkab).

def: s.m. A star (or stars) of gold, silver, or tinsel, &c. (worn as an ornament or insignia of rank).

ety: inf. n. of قول 'to speak; to say,' &c.
def: s.m. Speech, word, saying, dictum; assertion, declaration, affirmation; promise, agreement, stipulation, compact, consent, acquiescence; vow, resolve; — a kind of song
subs: (-ko), To pledge (one's) word (to); to promise; to vow, &c. (syn. baćan denā)
(with gen.), To go back from (one's) word, to break one's word or promise
s.m. Mutual agreement, compact, contract, bargain, convention, treaty, league
(-se), To make an agreement or compact (with), to stipulate, covenant; to make terms (with); to bind by contract
or qaul-kā saććā, adj. True to (one's) word, faithful to an engagement, &c.
s.m. A finger-ring representing two hands joined together
(-se), To pledge one's word (to); to make an agreement (with), &c. ( = qaul-qarār karnā)
(-kā), To take (one's) promise; to obtain the promise or consent, &c. (of)
s.m. A written agreement; a treaty, articles (of peace, &c.; — syn. iqrār-nāma, q.v.)
(-ko), To pledge (one's) word (to), to promise; to agree; to make terms; — to break (one's) word or promise
s.m. A simple declaration or affirmation; mere word or words
(-ke), According to the saying or dictum (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَول (qawl), from Arabic قَوْل (qawl).

def: s.m. = kaval, q.v.
s.m. = kauṅlā = kamlā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to a family, ancestral; — sprung from a high or noble family, of good family, well-born (syn. kulīn); — a man of good family, &c.
s.m. The breast, bosom; lap; embrace; — cavity, hollow, niche, recess, corner or angle (of a room, &c.); a side wall; — an armful, as much as can be embraced in the two arms (see kaulī).
s.m. The colic, a violent griping in the colon.
s.m. Name of one of the astronomical Karaṇs.

ety: S. कोल, or क्रोड+इका; see kaulā
def: s.f. Bosom; lap; embrace, grasp of the arms; armful, as much (of anything) as can be grasped in the fold of the arms; a bundle or sheaf of corn given as a perquisite to a reaper or a village servant
subs: (-kī), To take or fold (one) in the arms, to embrace; — to be grasping or greedy.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Belonging to a family or race, ancestral; customary in a family; hereditary; — s.m. A weaver; — a heretic; an impostor.
adj. (f. -ā), Peculiar or belonging to a noble family or high birth; — s.m. Report, rumour, gossip.
s.f. A small mouthful, a morsel.

ety: S. कोल, or क्रोड+इका; see kaulā
def: s.f. Bosom; lap; embrace, grasp of the arms; armful, as much (of anything) as can be grasped in the fold of the arms; a bundle or sheaf of corn given as a perquisite to a reaper or a village servant
subs: (-kī), To take or fold (one) in the arms, to embrace; — to be grasping or greedy.

def: s.f. A people, nation; a tribe, race, family; sect, caste
subs: adj. Of good family or race
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قَوْم (qawm).

def: s.f. corr. of qaum, q.v.

adj. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to a youth or a young girl; youthful, juvenile; girlish; maidenly; — relating to abstinence; — relating, or peculiar to, the god of war; — s.m. Childhood, youth; maidenhood.
s.f. One of the seven divine Mātr̤is or Mothers, or personified energies of the gods; the śakti or energy of Kārttikeya, the god of war.
s.f. corr. of kumārī, q.v.
adj. Of or relating to a people, or nation, &c.; national; tribal, &c.

ety: for A. قومية, abst. s. fr. qaumī
def: s.f. Connexion, the being of the same race or tribe, &c.; clanship; nationality, the belonging to some particular people or nation.
1˚ corr. of kumodak, q.v.; — 2˚ contrac. of kaumodakī, q.v.
s.f. The club or mace of Vishṇu or Krishṇa (given to him by Varuṇa).
s.f. The club or mace of Vishṇu or Krishṇa (given to him by Varuṇa).
s.f. corr. of kaumudī, q.v.
s.m. The month Kārttik (Oct.-Nov.).
s.f. Moonlight, moonshine (from its causing the kumuda or lotus to blossom); (met.) elucidation; — the day of full moon in the month Kārttik.
s.m. Being, existence; essence, entity; nature, the universe
subs: or kaun-o-makān, s.m. The world, the universe; every created being.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

def: (dialec.), 1˚ = ko (the case-sign of the dat. & acc., q.v.); — 2˚ = kā (the case-sign of the gen.); — 3˚ corr. of kaun, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

ety: Ap. Prk. कवणु, or कवडु; Prk. केद्दहो; S. कीदृशः
def: pron. Who? which? what? whether? (the formative of the sing. is kis; that of the plur. kin or kinh; but in Hindī poetry, in Braj, and in rustic speech kaun is seldom inflected; thus we find kaun-kau instead of kis-kau ( = kis-kā), &c.)
subs: Which? what? which (or what) particular one? what sort or manner of? — kaun-hī (Braj. kawan-hī, or kawan-hū), emphat forms of kaun (see hī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

def: s.m. The two worlds, both worlds; both kinds of beings (the spiritual and corporeal), men and angels.

ety: corr. of P. kafća, through a form kavća
def: s.m. A spoon, a ladle, a skimmer; the iron hook of a grain-parcher, &c.
s.f. = H کوندا कौंदा kauṅdā, s.m. = kauṅdh and kauṅdhā, qq.v.
s.m. = H کوند कौंद kauṅd, s.f. = kauṅdh and kauṅdhā, qq.v.

ety: S. चक्षु?+अन्ध or अन्धी; of. ćauṅdhī
def: s.f. Flash (of lightning, &c.); brightness, brilliance, splendour, dazzling light.
s.m. Lightning.
v.n. To flash, to glitter; to lighten
v.n. = kauṅdhnā, q.v.
adj. Existent; material; worldly.
s.m. A large and sweet species of orange, Citrus aurantium.
pron. See s.v. kaun.
s.f. Counsel; council
subs: To consult together, to hold a consultation; — to plot, intrigue.

def: s.m. Counsellor; a barrister.
s.m. Privity, a privy part; — s.f. A small piece of cloth worn over the privities, the langoṭī of a Hindū faqīr.
s.m. A descendant of Kuru; the Kauravas
subs: (S. kaurava+pāṇḍava), s.m. The Kauravas and Pāṇḍavas (two ancient families descended from Kurn through their respective fathers Dhritarāshtra and Pāṇḍu).
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: adj. A dialec. or rustic form of korā, q.v.
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: s.f.(?), Plenty, increase, abundance
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: s.m. A large shell or cowrie (see kauṛī); — kauṛe, s.m. pl. Cowries, money, wealth; price
subs: (-ko), To turn into cowries or cash; to sell; to sell cheap.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to Kuru, or to the Kurus; — a descendant of Kuru.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कौरव (kaurava).

def: (S. कौरवानि), s.m. pl. The Kaurav princes.
s.f. = کوڙي kauṛī, q.v.

s.f. A small shell (used as a coin), a 'cowrie,' Cyprœa moneta; — money, wealth; earnings; price; fare, hire; — a gland; — the breast-bone, the point of the sternum or ensiform cartilage (so called from its fancied resemblance to a cowrie); — a white speck at the root of a finger-nail
subs: or kauṛī-kauṛī-bhar, Every cowrie, full amount up to the last cowrie
(or ćukānā, or denā), To pay every cowrie, to pay in full
To receive every cowrie, to be paid in full
(or ḥairān honā), To be pinched (or put to one's wit's end) for every cowrie; to be in great distress; to be reduced to beggary; to become a pauper
(-se), To exact every cowrie (from); to receive the full amount up to the last cowrie
To make not the least inquiry after; not to value at a cowrie; to hold in no esteem
To be (selling) at the rate of three for a cowrie; to be held very cheap
Not worth a cowrie, worthless, good-for-nothing
Purchased for a few cowries, very cheap: phūṭī kauṛī, or kānī kauṛī, or jhinjī kauṛī, s.f. A broken, or perforated, or rubbed and worn, cowrie; a bad cowrie; a bit of money of the lowest value
(-kī), 'To reduce (one's) honour to the value of two cowries'; to dishonour, disgrace; — to make (oneself) cheap.

def: adj. Covered with, or wearing, cowries
subs: s.m. A slave who wears cowries on his ears and wrists as a mark of slavery.

def: adj. & s.m. contrac. of next, q.v.

ety: kauṛī, q.v. + Prk. आलओ = S. आल+कः; — or = kauṛī+wālā, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Rich, wealthy; — marked like a cowrie, braided; spotted; — a rich man; — a kind of braided, or spotted, snake; — a kingfisher; a braided or spotted bird, or flower (syn. koṛ-wālā).
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī) = kaurav.
s.f. A bow; arc (of a circle); curve (of an arch); the sign of Sagittarius
subs: The interior curve (of an arch), intrados; — the concave side
The exterior curve (of an arch), extrados; — the convex side

def: adj. = کوش kauś, q.v.; — s.m. (dialec.) = kos or koś.
adj. (f. -ī), Made of kuśa-grass; — s.m. The town of Kuśa,' the ancient city of Kanyā-kubja or Kanauj.
adj. (f. -ī), Silken, silk.
s.m. Well-being, welfare, good fortune, prosperity, happiness; a prosperous or fortunate event; — skilfulness, skill, cleverness, ability, capability; experience; device, stratagem.
s.f. Friendly inquiry, greeting, salutation; a respectful present (syn. naẕr).
s.m. Well-being, &c. ( = kauśal, q.v.).
s.f. The daughter of a prince of the Kośalas; — the wife of Daśaratha, and mother of Rāma.
s.m. Name of a fountain and river in Paradise (whence all the other rivers are supposed to derive their source).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَوثَر (kawsar), from Arabic كَوْثَر (kawṯar). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi کوثر (kvs̱r /⁠kausar⁠/).

def: adj. & s.m. Silken, of silk; — a silk; a silk cloth or garment.
adj. Made of kuśa-grass.

ety: fr. P. kavsh or kaush, q.v.
def: s.f. A small slipper; a woman's slipper.
adj. Bow-shaped; arched; curved; vaulted; of the form, or the colour, of the rainbow.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of the family of Kuśika; — (patronym.) a descendant of Kuśika; — an owl; an ichneumon
subs: s.m. or kauśik-gotrī, s.f. The race of Kuśika.

def: s.f. = kauśikī, or kosī, qq.v.
s.f. The river Kosī (in Bihār).
adv. = kaisā, and kaise, qq.v.
s.m. Name of a celebrated jewel worn by Kr̤ishṇa on his breast (it is believed to have been obtained along with thirteen other precious things at the churning of the ocean).
adj. Dyed with safflower; orange; red.

r: kauth, ko, kaun, kyā, arth, lit., tū gadhī kumhār-kī, tujhe Rām-se kauth
def: (prob. contrac. fr. ko = kaun or kyā+arth), s.m. lit. 'What business? What (have you) to do (with)?' — business; connection, relationship (e.g. tū gadhī kumhār-kī, tujhe Rām-se kauth, 'You are a potter's she-ass, what have you to do with Rām?').
adj. (Braj) = kaun-sā, q.v.s.v. kaun.
s.m. Curiosity, vehement desire, eagerness (syn. kautuk).

s.m. Vehement desire (for); eagerness, vehemence, impatience; festivity, gaiety; a festival; a show, spectacle, scene; song; dance; the ceremony with the marriage thread or ring (preceding a marriage); the marriage thread or ring; — joy, pleasure, happiness, enjoyment; sport, pastime, diversion; trick, prank; artifice.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Festive, gay; jocose; sportive; of pastime or diversion, contributing to diversion (by the exhibition of tumbling tricks, &c.); fond of, or given to, tricks or pranks, mischievous; — one who diverts, a jester; a tumbler, mountebank; a dancer, &c.
s.m. Relationship, affinity.
adj. Belonging to a family; constituting a family; having relations; — s.m. The father or master of a family.
adj. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to Kuver, coming from Kuver
subs: s.m. The region of Kuver, the northern quarter or point of the compass.

ety: fr. kāwīdan = kābīdan = kāfīdan = kāftan; rt. Zend kap; said to be akin to S. श्वि; cf. Gr. κόπ˚, κάπ˚, and Lat. cav˚ (as in cavare, cavus)
def: part. (used in comp.), Digging, excavating, hollowing out; — examining, investigating; meditating (on); — kāw or (more com.) kāw-kāw, s.f. Diligent search, strict investigation or inquiry, scrutiny; diligence, pains, toil, labour, trouble.
s.m. = کاوه kāwa, q.v.

ety: prob. for orig. kāwak, fr. kāw, q.v.
def: s.m. Ringing or lounging (a horse); — a crafty scheme; craft, subtlety
subs: (-ko, To ring or lounge (a horse), &c.; — to deceive, trick.

def: s.m. Armour, mail, coat of mail, cuirass; — an amulet, a charm (part of a mantra, or a mystical verse or verses, usually written on birch-leaf, kept about the person as a charm); — a drum (used in battle), a kettle-drum; — the tree Hibiscus populneoides
subs: s.m. The leaf of a birch-tree (which, inscribed with mystical verses, serves as an amulet, &c.).

def: s.m. pl. (of kāfir), Infidels.
(a barbarous pl. of kāg̠aẕ, after the model of the Arabic), s.m. pl. Papers; documents ( = kāg̠aẕāt).
s.m. A grammar for beginners, an elementary grammar
subs: adj. & s.m. Well versed in grammar
v.n. To undergo drill; to be drilled or exercised
(-ko), To teach (recruits) the drill exercise, to drill (soldiers)
v.n. To practise military evolutions, &c.; to be drilled, or exercised
s.f. A place for drill, &c.; a parade-ground
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قَوَاعِد (qawāʕid), plural of قَاعِدَة (qāʕida).

def: s.m. A fungus; mushroom; — a mouthful ( = kaval, q.v.).
adj. Hollow, empty (within); tending to decay, rotten inside; cracked; useless; awkward; presumptuous.
s.f. Hollowness, emptiness, &c.
s.m. pl. (of kaukab), Stars; constellations.

r: kāwal, kāʼol, kāwalī, kāʼolī, gasht, kāwal-gar
def: s.m. Patrol, watch; watchman.
s.m. A mouthful, a morsel; — unripe corn eaten (or swallowed as a mouthful) out of the ear; — an astringent wash for cleansing the mouth; a gargle; — a kind of fish.

r: kāwalī, kāʼolī, kāwal, kāʼol, gasht, kāwal-gar
def: s.m. Patrol, watch; watchman.

ety: v.n. fr. قوم 'to stand; to be erect,' &c.
def: s.m. Justice, equity; truth; consistency.
pron. = kaun, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

def: s.m. = kabandh, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of qānūn, q.v.), Canons, rules, laws, ordinances, statutes, &c.
s.f. = kāṅwar, q.v.
s.f. = kāṅwar, q.v.
s.m. A braid or fillet of hair.
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: s.m. = kawal or kaul, q.v.
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: s.m. The consonants of the guttural class collectively (viz. क, ख, ग, घ, ङ).
s.m. = kāṅwarī.
v.t. (dialec.) To pull up by the roots.
s.f. A kind of flat fish, Cojus cobojus (remarkable for going by land from one spot to another).
s.f. Turmeric; — a courtezan, harlot; — name of a river in the Deccan (Dakkhin).

ety: v.n. fr. قوي 'to be robust, or strong,' &c.
def: adj. Robust, firm, solid, strong, powerful, mighty, vigorous
subs: adj. Of strong or vigorous mind; high-spirited
adj. Of powerful destiny, fortunate, prosperous
adj. Of mighty frame, gigantic; strong-bodied, powerful, robust
adj. Of huge, or mighty, form or size, &c. ( = qawi-jus̤s̤a, q.v.).

def: s.m. A poet; a sage, a wise or learned man (cf. kab); — s.f. The bit of a bridle
subs: or kavi-rāʼe, or kavīśvar (˚vi+īś˚), s.m. Prince of poets; an eminent poet; — chief of sages
The poet of the Rāmāyaṇ, i.e. Vālmīkī.
ex: Please give some water to my elder brother.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کوں (kvṉ /⁠kū̃⁠/)., from Old Hindi काहू (kāhū), काहु (kāhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit कक्षे (kákṣe, “in the armpit”), locative singular of कक्ष (kákṣa, “armpit”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *káṭṣas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *káćšas, from Proto-Indo-European *kóḱsos (“joint”). Compare Deccani کو (kū), Braj कौ (kau), Assamese -ক (-k). The semantic development of the terms w…

def: s.f. The bit of a bridle; — a poetess (see kavi).
s.f. Digging; excavating; ploughing; examining, investigating, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. Digging; excavation; — investigation, inquiry, research, meditation; intentness; — menacing; disputing, fighting; enmity.
s.m. (dialec.), The wood-apple ( = kaith, q.v.).
s.m. (dialec.) = kāwit, and kaith, qq.v.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) = kaith, q.v.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) = kaith, q.v.
s.f. Poesy, poetry, the poetic art (see kabitāʼī).
s.f. = kavitā, q.v.
s.m. Poesy, poetry, a poem (applied to certain metrical compositions in Hindī and Braj); a particular species of verse (in Hindī poetry).
s.m. = kavitā, q.v.
